mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Linode's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free way to run Kubernetes without managing the underlying hardware. Key trade-offs include limited customization compared to self-managed clusters and potential costs associated with cloud resources.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Linode CLI, which is necessary for managing your Linode resources from the command line.

```bash

curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/linode/linode-cli/releases/latest | grep 'browser_download_url' | grep 'linux' | cut -d '"' -f 4 | xargs curl -O

tar -xzf linode-cli-* && sudo mv linode-cli /usr/local/bin/

linode-cli configure

```

> Download the latest Linode CLI for Linux.
> Extract and move the CLI to your PATH.
> Configure the CLI with your Linode API token.
commands: curl -s https://api.github.com/repos/linode/linode-cli/releases/latest | grep 'browser_download_url' | grep 'linux' | cut -d '"' -f 4 | xargs curl -O

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version and node count.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-create --label my-k8s-cluster --region us-east --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to connect to your new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster > ~/.kube/config

```

> Ensure you replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/linode/linode-cloud-controller-manager/v0.9.4/deploy/ccm-linode-template.yaml

```

> This applies the necessary configurations for the Linode Cloud Controller Manager.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

uninstall.title: Remove the Linode CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Linode CLI from your system.

```bash

sudo rm /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> This command deletes the Linode CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. Then, upgrade the cluster and verify the upgrade.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.
> Verify that all nodes are in the 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig file or the cluster's API server certificate.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the kubeconfig file using 'linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster > ~/.kube/config'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in default namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are no resources deployed in the default namespace.

**Fix:**
Deploy resources using 'kubectl apply -f <your-resource-file.yaml>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated to meet the demands of the workloads.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or upgrade the node types to larger instances.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create service: Error creating load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the Linode Cloud Controller Manager or the Linode API.

**Fix:**
Check the Linode API status and ensure the Cloud Controller Manager is correctly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Linode regions and improved integration with Linode's API.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with their statuses as 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Linode CLI installed and configured with your API token.
